title: Nasturtium microtites
taxonomy: Nasturtium microtites is a species within the genus Tropaeolum, which belongs to the family Tropaeolaceae. This plant is native to South America, but it has been naturalized in other parts of the world. The classification of Nasturtium microtites is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae. Its classification is also based on the APG III system of plant taxonomy, which groups it within the clade Angiospermophyta. This plant's classification is essential in understanding its evolutionary history and relationships with other species.

preparations: Nasturtium microtites is not a valid species of the Nasturtium plant. However, Nasturtium officinale and Nasturtium microphyllum have been used in herbal preparations. side_effects: Nasturtium microtites, also known as Nasturtium officinale, are not commonly reported to have significant side effects when consumed in moderation.
